summary: Six psalms of integrity, waiting, imprecation, and trust — ranging from David's declaration of innocence at the altar (Psalm 26) to the sweeping claim that all human beings together are lighter than a breath on the balances (Psalm 62).
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
26Individual Lament / VindicationDavidSurrounded by deceivers and evildoers; David protests his integrity and asks God to test and vindicate himWalking in integrity; refusing to associate with the wicked while loving God's sanctuary; liturgical devotion as expression of inward purity"I wash my hands in innocence and go around your altar, O LORD, proclaiming thanksgiving aloud." (v.6)Christ as the truly innocent one who alone prays this psalm without hypocrisy; his vindication at the resurrection
40Individual Thanksgiving / Lament (composite)DavidRescue from deep distress followed immediately by a new crisis; the experience of being saved and yet still needing salvationPatient waiting on God; joyful response to rescue; the primacy of obedience over sacrifice; delight in doing God's will"I delight to do your will, O my God; your law is within my heart." (v.8)Hebrews 10:5–7 quotes vv.6–8 as Christ's words at the incarnation: coming to do the Father's will and fulfill what the entire sacrificial system only shadowed
58ImprecatoryDavidCorrupt rulers and judges who pervert justice; the wicked going astray from birthThe injustice of wicked rulers vs. God's certain judgment of the earth; confidence that the righteous will be vindicated"Surely there is a reward for the righteous; surely there is a God who judges on earth." (v.11)Romans 3:13 quotes v.3; Christ as the righteous judge who will fully vindicate the oppressed at the last day; imprecations as prayers for justice handed over to God
61Individual Lament / TrustDavidCrisis experienced from a far place ("the end of the earth"); possibly the Absalom exile; faintness and distance from God's houseGod as the rock higher than the psalmist; refuge under his wings; intercession for the king's enduring reign"Lead me to the rock that is higher than I, for you have been my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy." (vv.2–3)Christ as the eternal Rock and King in whom the prayer for an unending reign (v.6) is perfectly and permanently fulfilled
62Trust / ConfidenceDavidTreacherous enemies who seek to topple the psalmist from his position while speaking blessings and harboring cursesSoul-silence before God as the only rock; the total vanity of human beings on the balances; power and steadfast love belonging to God alone"For God alone my soul waits in silence; from him comes my salvation. He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress." (vv.1–2)Christ who perfectly entrusted himself to the Father; Philippians 4:7 (the peace of God) echoes this posture of soul-silence; the vanity of all human weight answered in Christ who is the same yesterday, today, and forever
64Individual LamentDavidSecret schemes of sharp-tongued enemies who plot in hiding and encourage one another in their maliceThe inevitable reversal: plotters undone by their own devices; God's arrow finding those who hide; the righteous taking refuge and rejoicing"But God shoots his arrow at them; they are wounded suddenly." (v.7)The cross as the supreme reversal of plotting; final judgment when all hidden schemes are exposed; the pattern of lament turned to praise repeated in resurrection
